a x y : @MulOpposite α

    @SemiconjBy (@MulOpposite α) (@MulOpposite.instMul α inst✝) a x y

by
  have h₁ : SemiconjBy (unop a) (unop y) (unop x) ↔ (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) := by
    simp [SemiconjBy]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  have h₂ : SemiconjBy a x y ↔ a * x = y * a := by
    simp [SemiconjBy]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  have h₃ : (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) ↔ a * x = y * a := by
    constructor
    · -- Prove the forward direction: if (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a), then a * x = y * a
      intro h
      have h₄ : (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) := h
      have h₅ : unop a * unop y = unop x * unop a := by simpa using h₄
      have h₆ : unop (a * x) = unop (y * a) := by
        calc
          unop (a * x) = unop a * unop x := by
            simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
            <;> simp_all [unop_op]
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> simp_all [unop_op]
          _ = unop a * unop x := by rfl
          _ = unop y * unop a := by
            calc
              unop a * unop x = unop a * unop x := by rfl
              _ = unop y * unop a := by
                have h₇ : unop a * unop y = unop x * unop a := by simpa using h₅
                have h₈ : unop a * unop x = unop y * unop a := by
                  calc
                    unop a * unop x = unop a * unop x := by rfl
                    _ = unop y * unop a := by
                      -- Use the fact that unop is a homomorphism and the given equation to derive the result
                      calc
                        unop a * unop x = unop a * unop x := by rfl
                        _ = unop y * unop a := by
                          -- Here we need to use the properties of the opposite multiplication
                          -- and the fact that unop is a homomorphism to derive the result
                          have h₉ : unop a * unop y = unop x * unop a := by simpa using h₅
                          have h₁₀ : unop a * unop x = unop y * unop a := by
                            calc
                              unop a * unop x = unop a * unop x := by rfl
                              _ = unop y * unop a := by
                                -- Use the fact that unop is a homomorphism and the given equation to derive the result
                                simp_all [unop_op, MulOpposite.mul_def]
                                <;>
                                (try ring_nf at *) <;>
                                (try simp_all [unop_op]) <;>
                                (try aesop)
                                <;>
                                (try simp_all [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm])
                                <;>
                                (try ring_nf at *)
                                <;>
                                (try simp_all [unop_op])
                                <;>
                                (try aesop)
                          exact h₁₀
                exact h₈
              _ = unop y * unop a := by rfl
          _ = unop (y * a) := by
            simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
            <;> simp_all [unop_op]
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> simp_all [unop_op]
      have h₇ : a * x = y * a := by
        have h₈ : unop (a * x) = unop (y * a) := h₆
        have h₉ : a * x = y * a := by
          apply Eq.symm
          apply Eq.symm
          apply unop_inj.mp
          simp_all [unop_op]
          <;>
          aesop
        exact h₉
      exact h₇
    · -- Prove the reverse direction: if a * x = y * a, then (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a)
      intro h
      have h₄ : a * x = y * a := h
      have h₅ : unop (a * x) = unop (y * a) := by
        rw [h₄]
      have h₆ : unop a * unop x = unop y * unop a := by
        calc
          unop a * unop x = unop (a * x) := by
            simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
            <;> simp_all [unop_op]
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> simp_all [unop_op]
          _ = unop (y * a) := by rw [h₅]
          _ = unop y * unop a := by
            simp [MulOpposite.mul_def]
            <;> simp_all [unop_op]
            <;> ring_nf
            <;> simp_all [unop_op]
      have h₇ : (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) := by
        calc
          (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop a) * (unop y) := by rfl
          _ = (unop x) * (unop a) := by
            have h₈ : unop a * unop x = unop y * unop a := h₆
            calc
              (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop a) * (unop y) := by rfl
              _ = (unop x) * (unop a) := by
                -- Use the fact that unop is a homomorphism and the given equation to derive the result
                have h₉ : unop a * unop x = unop y * unop a := h₆
                simp_all [unop_op, MulOpposite.mul_def]
                <;>
                (try ring_nf at *) <;>
                (try simp_all [unop_op]) <;>
                (try aesop)
                <;>
                (try simp_all [mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm])
                <;>
                (try ring_nf at *)
                <;>
                (try simp_all [unop_op])
                <;>
                (try aesop)
          _ = (unop x) * (unop a) := by rfl
      exact h₇
  
  have h₄ : SemiconjBy (unop a) (unop y) (unop x) ↔ SemiconjBy a x y := by
    rw [h₁]
    rw [h₂]
    rw [h₃]
    <;>
    aesop
  
  exact h₄

  have h₁ : SemiconjBy (unop a) (unop y) (unop x) ↔ (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) := by
    simp [SemiconjBy]
  have h₂ : SemiconjBy a x y ↔ a * x = y * a := by
    simp [SemiconjBy]
  have h₃ : (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) ↔ a * x = y * a := by
    simp [mul_comm, ← MulOpposite.mul_eq_mul_unop]
    <;>
    simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_eq_mul_unop]
    <;>
    simp_all [mul_comm, mul_assoc, mul_left_comm]
  simp_all [h₁, h₂, h₃]

  constructor
  · -- Prove the forward direction: (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a) → a * x = y * a
    intro h
    have h₁ : (unop a : α) * unop y = unop x * unop a := h
    have h₂ : unop (a * x) = unop (y * a) := by
      -- Use the properties of unop and the multiplication in MulOpposite to show the equality
      calc
        unop (a * x) = unop (a * x) := rfl
        _ = unop x * unop a := by
          simp [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
        _ = unop x * unop a := by rfl
        _ = (unop a : α) * unop y := by rw [h₁]
        _ = unop (y * a) := by
          simp [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> ring_nf
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
    -- Since unop is injective, we can deduce that a * x = y * a
    have h₃ : a * x = y * a := by
      apply MulOpposite.unop_inj.mp
      exact h₂
    exact h₃
  · -- Prove the reverse direction: a * x = y * a → (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a)
    intro h
    have h₁ : a * x = y * a := h
    have h₂ : (unop a : α) * unop y = unop x * unop a := by
      -- Use the properties of unop and the multiplication in MulOpposite to show the equality
      calc
        (unop a : α) * unop y = unop y * unop a := by
          simp [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop] at h₁ ⊢
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> ring_nf at *
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> linarith
        _ = unop (a * x) := by
          simp [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> ring_nf at *
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> linarith
        _ = unop (y * a) := by
          rw [h₁]
        _ = unop x * unop a := by
          simp [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> ring_nf at *
          <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul_def, unop]
          <;> linarith
    -- Since unop is injective, we can deduce that (unop a) * (unop y) = (unop x) * (unop a)
    exact h₂

by
  dsimp [SemiconjBy] at *
  simp [unop_id, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm] at *
  <;>
  simp_all [unop_id, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
  <;>
  aesop

by
  dsimp [SemiconjBy]
  <;>
  (try { simp [Mul.mul, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm] }) <;>
  (try { simp_all [unop_inj, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm] }) <;>
  (try { aesop }) <;>
  (try { ring_nf }) <;>
  (try { simp_all [unop_inj, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm] }) <;>
  (try { aesop })
  <;>
  simp_all [Mul.mul, unop_inj, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
  <;>
  aesop
  <;>
  simp_all [Mul.mul, unop_inj, mul_assoc, mul_comm, mul_left_comm]
  <;>
  aesop

by
  dsimp [SemiconjBy]
  <;>
  (try simp_all [Mul.mul, unop_inj])
  <;>
  (try
    {
      cases a <;> cases x <;> cases y <;>
      simp_all [Mul.mul, unop_inj]
      <;>
      (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all)
      <;>
      (try aesop)
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      constructor <;> intro h <;>
      simp_all [Mul.mul, unop_inj] <;>
      (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all) <;>
      (try aesop)
    })
  <;>
  (try
    {
      simp_all [Mul.mul, unop_inj]
      <;>
      (try ring_nf at * <;> simp_all)
      <;>
      (try aesop)
    })

by
  simp [SemiconjBy, MulOpposite.mul, mul_assoc]
  <;>
  (try cases a <;> cases x <;> cases y <;> simp_all [MulOpposite.mul, mul_assoc])
  <;>
  (try aesop)
  <;>
  (try
    {
      rw [← mul_assoc]
      <;>
      simp_all [MulOpposite.mul, mul_assoc]
      <;>
      aesop
    })
